zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn於大約1360年出生在英國藍卡爾布萊恩時，他的父親Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn27歲，母親Katharine Gerard24歲。他於1383年8月20日之前與Mrs Matilda Gerard在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1416年3月27日在英國爾金斯利享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn于大约1360年出生在英国藍卡爾布萊恩时，他的父亲Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn27岁，母亲Katharine Gerard24岁。他于1383年8月20日之前与Mrs Matilda Gerard在英国藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1416年3月27日在英国爾金斯利享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Enzo, Gaspare, Luigi, Rocco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Filippo, Francesco, Giovanni, Libero.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the ancient Germanic personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ). This surname is also found in southern France.
German and French (mainly Nord): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name German Gerhard , French Gérard (see Gerard ), using the genitive of the Latinized form Gerardus.

variants.name: Garrison
meanings: English: from a shortened form of Middle English Gerardson, a patronymic from the personal name Gerard + son, or an Anglicized form of its Dutch cognate Gerritsen . The English surname is probably in most cases of Flemish or Dutch origin. It was brought to Britain by immigrants from the Low Countries, especially in the 15th and 16th centuries.
Americanized form of Jewish   Gerson   (see   Gershon  ).
Americanized form of Dutch   Gerritsen  .

variants.name: Garrard
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Gerard  .
Altered form of French Gérard (see Gerard ).
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Geenen
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Geen, a pet form of Gerard .

variants.name: Gerardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Alberto, Alfredo, Aurelio, Carlos, Jesus, Juan, Norberto, Ramon, Tomas, Ana.  Italian Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Enrico, Marco, Marco Antonio.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: English: variant of   Gerard  .

variants.name: Geron
meanings: French (Géron): from the personal name Gero, Gerone, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, as for example Gérard (see Gerard ).
Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
English (Lancashire): of Norman origin, from the personal name Geron of ancient Germanic origin, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gherardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Alessandro, Angelo, Antonio, Donato, Egidio, Ennio, Enzo, Gildo, Lucio, Luigi, Primo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Gherardo, one of themany Italian equivalents of Gerard .

variants.name: Garard
meanings: English (London and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Gerard  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Bryn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Glamorgan and Monmouthshire) and English: topographic name from Welsh bryn ‘hill’, or a habitational name from any of several places so called in Wales, or in Cheshire or Lancashire.
Norwegian: habitational name from a farm name found in seven places, in Old Norse Brúvin ‘bridge’ + vin ‘meadow’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn was born about 1360, in Bryn, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, was 27 and his mother, Katharine Gerard, was 24. He married Mrs Matilda Gerard before 20 August 1383, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 27 March 1416, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn est né environ 1360, à Bryn, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Katharine Gerard, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Matilda Gerard avant 20 août 1383, à Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 27 mars 1416, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn um 1360 in Bryn, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn 27 und seine Mutter Katharine Gerard 24. Er hat vor 20. August 1383 in Lancashire, England Mrs Matilda Gerard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 27. März 1416 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Kingsley, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn è nato il circa 1360 nel Bryn, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Katharine Gerard, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Mrs Matilda Gerard prima del 20 agosto 1383, nel Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 27 marzo 1416, nel Kingsley, Cheshire, England, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Gerard of Brynが約1360年にイギリスランカシュアイアブリンで生まれた時，父Sir Peter Gerard of Brynは27歳，母Katharine Gerardは24歳であった。1383年8月20日以前にイギリスランカシュアイアでMrs Matilda Gerardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1416年3月27日にイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn(이)가 대략 1360년 영국 랭카실르 브린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Katharine Gerard(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1383년 8월 20일 이전 영국 랭카실르에서 Mrs Matilda Gerard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1416년 3월 27일 영국 체실르 킨그슬리에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Katharine Gerard, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs Matilda Gerard antes 20 de agosto de 1383, em Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de março de 1416, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn родился около 1360 г. в местности брин, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Katharine Gerard, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Matilda Gerard перед 20 августа 1383 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 27 марта 1416 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas Gerard of Bryn nació aproximadamente 1360, en Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Katharine Gerard, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Mrs Matilda Gerard antes de 20 de agosto de 1383, en Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 27 de marzo de 1416, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 57 años.
